edges.description: Without the activator cofactor, GM2 ganglioside cannot be degraded and accumulates.
edges.target: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration

edges.description: Neuronal GM2 lysosomal storage is the proximal lesion driving progressive neurodegeneration.
edges.source: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration

edges.description: Progressive neuronal storage and neurodegeneration cause loss of acquired developmental milestones.
edges.source: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration

edges.description: GM2 storage in retinal neurons produces the characteristic macular cherry-red spot.
edges.source: Neuronal GM2 Ganglioside Accumulation and Neurodegeneration
edges.target: Cherry red spot of the macula

edges.description: Neuronal storage disease can involve ocular motor pathways, producing nystagmus.

edges.description: Infantile neuronal storage and neurodegeneration produce reduced tone.

nodes.description: A macular cherry-red spot, as in other GM2 gangliosidoses.
nodes.id: Cherry red spot of the macula

evidence.reference_title: GM2 Activator Deficiency Caused by a Homozygous Exon 2 Deletion in GM2A.
evidence.snippet: The combination of a cherry red spot and developmental regression

nodes.description: Loss of acquired developmental milestones, as in classic Tay-Sachs disease.

evidence.reference_title: GM2 Activator Deficiency Caused by a Homozygous Exon 2 Deletion in GM2A.
evidence.snippet: The combination of a cherry red spot and developmental regression

nodes.description: Biallelic GM2A variants eliminate the GM2 activator protein (GM2AP), a non-enzymatic lysosomal cofactor essential for hexosaminidase A to access and hydrolyze GM2 ganglioside. The defect is mechanistically distinct from Tay-Sachs (HEXA) and Sandhoff (HEXB) disease, and enzyme activities are normal.

evidence.reference_title: GM2 gangliosidosis AB variant: first case of late onset and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: two mutations in the GM2A gene encoding the GM2 activator protein (GM2-AP), an essential co-factor of hexosaminidase A

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function GM2A variants causing GM2 activator protein deficiency

nodes.description: Increased sensitivity to sound (hyperacusis / exaggerated startle).

global

nodes.description: GM2 ganglioside accumulates in neuronal lysosomes because hexosaminidase A cannot degrade it without the activator, driving progressive neurodegeneration.
